scene_descriptions.page_description: A dashboard showing a list of research studies in a table format. A sidebar on the left provides navigation to various sections like Ask AI, Notifications, and Project Delivery.
visible_interactive_elements: Table headers (Title, Type, Status, Quota, Closing date, Created date, Created by)

scene_descriptions.page_description: The studies dashboard remains visible with the list of active and draft research projects.
The studies dashboard is shown with the cursor hovering over the 'Closing date' column header in the studies table.
A 'New study' dropdown menu is open, presenting options for different types of research studies to create.
The first step of the study creation wizard, where the user is prompted to enter a title for the new study.
visible_interactive_elements: Sidebar navigation (Study Setup, Audience, Additional Info, etc.)

scene_descriptions.page_description: The study title 'TV Streaming -' has been entered into the text input field.
The 'Device' step of the study setup, where the user selects which devices participants should use. 'Any device' is currently selected.
The 'Quota' step of the study setup, where the user specifies the number of participants needed. The value '5' is entered.
The 'Session duration' step of the study setup. A dropdown menu shows '45 mins' as the selected duration.
The session duration page remains visible with the 'Next' button highlighted.
The 'Participant selection' step, offering a choice between 'Hand picking' and 'Automated' selection. 'Hand picking' is currently selected.
The participant selection page remains visible with the cursor hovering over the 'Automated' selection card.
The 'Panel' step under the 'Audience' section. The user can choose to recruit from the 'Askable Panel' or 'Send a link to your list'. 'Askable Panel' is selected.
visible_interactive_elements: Send a link to your list selection card

scene_descriptions.page_description: The 'Demographic filters' step, allowing users to add basic filters and exclude previously contacted participants.
The 'Exclude people you've already spoken to' dropdown menu is open, showing various time-based exclusion options.
The 'Don't exclude anyone' option has been selected from the dropdown menu.
A modal is open to create a 'Multiple Choice' screener question. It includes fields for the question text and multiple answer options.
The user is typing a question into the 'Screener question' input field within the multiple choice modal.
The screener question and two answer options have been entered into the multiple choice modal.
Four answer options have now been entered into the multiple choice screener question modal.
The first custom screener question is saved and displayed on the page. Options to edit, duplicate, or delete the question are visible.

scene_descriptions.page_title: Select a study to copy from
scene_descriptions.page_description: A modal titled 'Select a study to copy from' is open, showing a list of previous research studies that can be used as templates.
The user has returned to the custom screener page after closing the copy study modal.
A new modal is open to create a 'Short answer' screener question.
The user is typing a short answer screener question into the input field.
The short answer screener question 'What is the name of your favourite TV show that you're wa' is being typed.
Two custom screener questions (one multiple choice and one short answer) are now saved and displayed on the page.
A third screener question modal (Multiple Choice) is opened to add another question.
The user is typing the third screener question into the multiple choice modal.
The third screener question 'Which tv streaming service are you using most right now?' is entered, and the user begins adding answer options.
The first answer option 'Netflix' is entered into the multiple choice screener modal.
Three answer options ('Netflix', 'Stan', 'Disney+') have been entered into the screener modal.
The multiple choice screener modal remains visible as the user prepares to add a fourth answer option.
A fourth answer option 'Prime Video' has been added to the screener question.
Three custom screener questions are now saved and displayed on the page.
The custom screener page remains visible with the three questions listed.

scene_descriptions.page_title: Short description of who you're looking for
scene_descriptions.page_description: The 'Description' step under 'Additional Info', where users provide short and full summaries of the research opportunity.
The 'Contacts' step, showing the assigned Organiser and Facilitator for the study.
The final review step of the study setup, showing a summary of all details and a mobile preview of the study for participants.
The study management dashboard for the newly created study. A banner indicates the study is currently in review. A participant table is visible but empty.
The 'Settings' page for the study, showing sections for special instructions, contact information, and legal terms.
